note: TASTE, not specification. Observations describe the QUALITY a choice produces and what causes it, so the feeling can be carried into a completely different brief and blended with other critiques. Mechanism — exact values, class names, libraries — deliberately lives in guidelines and primitives instead, because a page that copies mechanism copies the site, and a page that borrows quality can become its own thing.

observations.what: The brand colour is the ground the whole page stands on, not a highlight sprinkled through it. You are inside the colour rather than looking at it.
observations.why: Committing a colour to the field reads as ownership; using it as an accent reads as decoration. The feeling is a company that does not need to hedge. It also quietly settles every later colour question, which is why a long page stays coherent — there was never a second decision to get wrong.

observations.what: Bold and calm at the same time. The type is large and set with real confidence, yet nothing shouts — it asks for attention by being simple rather than by being loud.
observations.why: Loudness and confidence are usually confused. Loud means many competing signals; confident means one signal with nothing arguing against it. A page earns calm by removing competitors, not by softening what remains, and that combination is what makes something feel expensive rather than energetic.

observations.what: The page tells a story through the way it moves. Motion depicts what the company actually does rather than ornamenting it, so watching the page is a way of understanding the product.
observations.why: This is the difference between motion that is authored and motion that is applied. Movement chosen to mean something makes a page feel written; movement chosen because a section needed animating makes it feel decorated. Ask what the subject DOES, then move something the way that thing moves.
observations.carry: Find the verb in the brief and animate that, rather than fading a block in because it arrived.

observations.what: It feels like one continuous movement rather than a sequence of sections. The ground changes underneath you while you are moving, so you are carried through the page instead of stepped along it.
observations.why: Change that happens ACROSS a boundary joins two parts; change that happens AT a boundary separates them. Most pages announce every section and end up feeling like slides stapled together. Letting one motion run through the seam is what makes a long page feel like a single held thought.
observations.carry: Let a change begin before the boundary and finish after it, rather than aligning it to the edge.

observations.what: You can see how well made it is. The care is legible in small deliberate imperfections — repeating elements that are close but never identical, so nothing snaps into a mechanical loop.
observations.why: Perfect repetition reads as generated; near-repetition reads as made. A tiny refusal to line up is the cheapest and most reliable signal of authorship, and a viewer registers it as quality long before they could say why.
observations.carry: When something repeats, vary it slightly and irregularly rather than cloning it.

observations.what: Emptiness is used as composition, not left as a gap. Large areas hold nothing at all, but the type is anchored against an edge so the void has a shape and a direction to be read from.
observations.why: Empty space reads as intentional when something is placed decisively against it, and as a mistake when content floats in the middle of it. The same amount of nothing can be the best or the worst thing on a page depending entirely on whether an edge is doing work.
observations.carry: If a section is mostly empty, anchor its content hard to one edge — never centre it in the void.

observations.what: Everything ornamental is drawn from the subject matter itself, so decoration and meaning are the same thing instead of two competing ideas.
observations.why: Applied ornament always eventually fights the content, because it came from somewhere else. Ornament derived from the subject cannot fight it. This is also the honest answer for a product with nothing photogenic to show: make the graphic FROM what the thing does rather than borrowing a stock image of people at laptops.

observations.what: The structure is fields rather than boxes. Areas of colour define where you are; nothing is packaged into a card with a border and a corner radius.
observations.why: Cards are containers for things a designer has not decided how to relate. Fields state that the whole region belongs together. Boxes also multiply — once one exists, everything becomes one — whereas a field stays a field, which is why the page never turns into a grid of tiles.

observations.what: The motion budget is spent almost entirely on two things: one focal graphic and the feel of scrolling itself. Individual elements are largely left alone.
observations.why: Scroll feel is felt continuously by every visitor for the whole visit; a per-element entrance is noticed once, if at all. Concentrating the budget where attention actually is buys far more perceived quality than distributing it evenly, and it is why the page feels alive without feeling busy.

throughline: Authority through subtraction, and motion that means something. The brand colour is the ground rather than an accent, the type is bold and calm at once, and the page moves as one continuous thing — the ground changing underneath you while you travel rather than announcing each new section. Movement depicts what the company does instead of decorating it, ornament is drawn from the subject so decoration and meaning never compete, and the craft is legible in small deliberate irregularities that stop anything snapping into a mechanical loop. What to carry into an unrelated brief is not the blue or the lines: it is that confidence is one signal with nothing arguing against it, that emptiness becomes composition the moment something is anchored decisively against it, and that a page feels made rather than generated when its repetitions refuse to be identical.
